sanskrit: पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्य त्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान्। न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्यो लोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव॥
transliteration: pitāsi lokasya carācarasya tvam asya pūjyaś ca gurur garīyān | na tvat-samo ’sty abhyadhikaḥ kuto ’nyo loka-traye ’py apratima-prabhāva ||
literal_meaning: You are the father of the moving and non-moving world; You are its worshipable one, the preceptor, the weightiest; none equal to You exists, how then another greater? In the three worlds Your power is without comparison.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences total ego-dissolution before the cosmic form: every relational schema collapses into one archetype—Ultimate Parent, Guru, and Unsurpassable Ideal—triggering awe, surrender, and a felt loss of personal agency.
psychological_state: Transient ego-boundary dissolution; shift from agentic self to devotional self-schema; potential for post-traumatic growth if integrated.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving CEO survives a near-death experience, suddenly perceives the vastness of the universe, and feels both microscopic and held by something immeasurably greater than any boardroom victory.
anchor_text: Bow to the One who out-measures every measure.
